focus: Probe the reality of time. Use this exchange between Joe Rogan and physicist Michelle Thaller as the seed: ROGAN: "The weirdest thing that I have ever heard anybody say is that all time exists currently." THALLER: "That is Albert Einstein." ROGAN: "When we measure time what exactly are we measuring? When we create a clock that runs 24 hours per day what is it measuring?" THALLER: "That is a deep question. That question caused everything in physics to fall apart." ROGAN: "I still do not understand what we are measuring." THALLER: "I do not think I have an answer for you. I do not think anybody does." Codex and Claude should each generate one original finding that takes this admission of physics ignorance seriously. Possible angles: what contemplative traditions saw about time that physics now circles back to; whether the block universe makes the felt present an illusion or a perspectival fact; what a clock actually measures if time is not a flowing river; whether Dogen being-time, Plotinus on eternity, or Augustine on the distended soul offers something operational physics has not yet operationalized. Ground each finding in real primary sources, propose a falsifiable prediction, name the crux. Follow docs/writing-style.md. No em dashes.
notes: Generated one draft idea record in a parallel run.
Updated doctrine candidates: 1 teaching, 1 practice, 4 test.
